data.title: Dycom projects up to $7.15B revenue for FY 2027 as Power Solutions acquisition drives diversification
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Dycom Industries, Inc. (DY) Q4 2026

Management View

  • CEO Daniel Peyovich stated that "Dycom's fourth quarter results are an excellent finish to a record year as we set new benchmarks across nearly every financial metric we track." He highlighted that the company "exceeded the high end of our annual revenue outlook" and delivered record fourth quarter revenue of $1.46 billion, with organic revenue up 16.6% compared to the prior year. He emphasized the strategic acquisition of Power Solutions, noting it positions Dycom "at the intersection of digital infrastructure and the burgeoning data center market." Peyovich also pointed to a record $9.5 billion backlog, with $6.3 billion expected to be completed over the next 12 months, and a book-to-bill ratio of 1.3x.

  • Peyovich detailed that adjusted EBITDA reached $162.4 million for the quarter and non-GAAP adjusted diluted EPS was $2.03, a 42% increase year-over-year. He said, "Our approach to the pipeline remains disciplined. We are optimizing for high-value engagement that balances risk with superior returns."

  • CFO H. DeFerrari stated, "We delivered record annual results in fiscal 2026 with strong revenue growth, significant margin expansion and robust free cash flow." He reported that organic revenue grew 16.6% in Q4, with $95.8 million in acquired revenues from Power Solutions. DeFerrari added, "Consolidated adjusted EBITDA of $162.4 million increased 39.6% over Q4 '25."

Outlook

  • Dycom projects FY 2027 total revenue to be between $6.85 billion and $7.15 billion, representing growth of approximately 23.6% to 29% year-over-year, or 6.6% to 10.3% on an organic basis. CFO DeFerrari indicated, "For the Communications segment, we expect contract revenues to range from $5.70 billion to $5.90 billion... For the Building Systems segment, we expect contract revenues ranging from $1.15 billion to $1.25 billion."

  • Management outlined expectations for continued adjusted EBITDA margin expansion, with modest gains in Communications and mid-teens margins for Building Systems. Power Solutions is expected to drive 15% to 25% growth in the Building Systems segment.

  • For Q1 FY 2027, Dycom expects total contract revenues of $1.64 billion to $1.71 billion, adjusted EBITDA of $202 million to $218 million, and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.57 to $2.90.

Financial Results

  • Q4 revenue reached $1.46 billion, a 34.4% increase over Q4 FY 2025, with organic revenue up 16.6%. Adjusted EBITDA margin improved by 41 basis points to 11.1%. Non-GAAP adjusted diluted EPS was $2.03. Operating cash flow increased 27.7% to $419 million for the quarter, and DSOs improved by 13 days year-over-year to 101 days.

  • Full year revenue was $5.55 billion, up 17.9% from the prior year, with organic revenue up 6.5%. Non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA was $737.7 million with a margin of 13.3%. Free cash flow more than doubled to $435.3 million.

  • At quarter end, Dycom reported cash and equivalents of $709.2 million and total liquidity of $1.46 billion. Pro forma net leverage was 2.3x adjusted EBITDA, with a clear path to reduce to 2x over the next 12 months.

Q&A

  • Sangita Jain, KeyBanc Capital Markets Inc.: Asked about expansion plans for Power Solutions and cross-selling opportunities. CEO Peyovich responded that integration is proceeding well, highlighting early cross-sell success, particularly with hyperscalers, and outlined a 15% to 25% growth target for Power Solutions.

  • Eric Luebchow, Wells Fargo Securities, LLC: Inquired about the long-haul, middle mile, and inside defense market, previously estimated at $20 billion. CEO Peyovich stated, "We are getting more phone calls and seeing more opportunities than we saw even a quarter ago or frankly, even a week ago, the demand is that strong."

  • Frank Louthan, Raymond James & Associates: Asked about Power Solutions' growth rate and exposure to Echostar. Peyovich confirmed no exposure to Echostar and reaffirmed Power Solutions' 4-year CAGR of 15%, now targeting 15% to 25% for the coming year.

  • Michael Dudas, Vertical Research Partners: Questioned margin investment pace versus FY 2026. Peyovich explained margin growth will continue but at a slower pace than the prior year due to ongoing growth investments and labor additions.

  • Judah Aronovitz, UBS: Asked about Building Systems margin potential and SG&A. Peyovich described mid-teens as the right margin profile currently, with DeFerrari noting $18 million in transaction costs in SG&A for the quarter.

  • Richard Choe, JPMorgan: Requested clarification on hyperscale opportunity and acquisitions’ geographic focus. Peyovich said the company is not limited to DMV area and considers expansion into new, high-growth markets.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on growth rates, margin sustainability, and integration execution, reflecting a slightly positive but cautious tone, particularly regarding the scalability of Power Solutions and BEAD program timeline.

  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently highlighting disciplined growth, operational execution, and robust demand drivers. Peyovich repeatedly emphasized, "We feel really good about the growth profile" and pointed to strong customer response for cross-selling opportunities. There was some caution when discussing margin expansion pacing and labor investments.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone shifted from excitement about the acquisition to confidence in executing integration and scaling the new segment, with ongoing optimism about secular demand drivers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for FY 2027 revenue and margin expansion represents a higher growth trajectory than the prior quarter’s outlook, now including the impact of Power Solutions.

  • Strategic focus has shifted from acquisition announcement and integration planning (Q3) to operational execution, cross-sell, and margin discipline in Q4.

  • Analysts in the current quarter focused more heavily on Power Solutions integration, cross-selling synergies, margin sustainability, and labor force scalability compared to prior quarter’s questions on acquisition rationale and addressable market size.

  • Management’s confidence remains high, with a stronger emphasis on integration progress and capitalizing on digital infrastructure demand.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified potential industry-wide skilled labor shortages as a challenge, emphasizing ongoing investments in workforce development and training facilities.

  • Weather-related disruptions impacted margins in Q4. Seasonality and timing of funding for BEAD and other large-scale programs remain factors affecting revenue ramp.

  • Analysts inquired about margin drag from integration costs and headcount growth, with management highlighting ongoing discipline and investment in capabilities ahead of anticipated demand.

Final Takeaway

Dycom delivered record financial performance in Q4 and FY 2026, driven by robust organic growth, strategic entry into the data center market, and the integration of Power Solutions. Management projects significant revenue growth for FY 2027, with a disciplined focus on margin expansion, cash flow, and workforce investment to support intensifying demand drivers in digital infrastructure. The company is positioned to leverage its expanded platform for growth in both communications and building systems, supported by a record backlog and strengthening customer relationships across carriers and hyperscalers.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: UBS sees Accenture’s Ookla deal as timely bet on AI-driven network intelligence
data.description:
Accenture’s (ACN) planned acquisition of network analytics firm Ookla is a well timed move that could strengthen the consulting giant’s position in artificial intelligence and digital infrastructure, according to UBS analyst Kevin McVeigh.

He said the roughly $1.2 billion transaction reflects a strategic expansion of Accenture’s (ACN) data capabilities as enterprises invest heavily in AI driven systems.

“We look favorably on the timely transaction,” McVeigh wrote in a March 3 research note, pointing to the importance of network level data as artificial intelligence systems become more deeply embedded in digital infrastructure.

Ookla, which generated about $231 million in revenue in 2025, provides network intelligence and analytics tools including Speedtest and Downdetector. UBS estimates the acquisition price implies roughly 5.2 times sales.

Data advantage in AI era

McVeigh said Ookla’s products provide insights across networks, devices and applications that can help organizations manage increasingly complex digital systems.

That data could support a range of enterprise use cases, from fraud detection in banking to smart home analytics in utilities and customer traffic optimization in retail.

The analyst said integrating these capabilities into Accenture’s (ACN) consulting and technology services should strengthen its ability to help telecom operators, hyperscale cloud providers and large enterprises optimize Wi-Fi and 5G networks.

Larger deal but consistent with strategy

The acquisition stands out because it is larger than many of Accenture’s (ACN) typical transactions. The company has historically relied on smaller tuck in acquisitions rather than deals exceeding $1 billion.

Even so, McVeigh said the purchase aligns with Accenture’s (ACN) broader strategy of disciplined inorganic growth tied to cloud modernization, AI and industry specific digital solutions.

UBS expects acquisitions to contribute about 1.5% of Accenture’s (ACN) revenue growth in fiscal 2026, with the company planning roughly $3 billion in deal spending compared with about $1.5 billion the prior year.

AI fears may be overdone

McVeigh also framed the acquisition as a response to investor concerns that artificial intelligence could reduce demand for consulting services.

In his view, the market may be underestimating Accenture’s (ACN) ability to benefit from AI adoption rather than be disrupted by it.

data.title: Europe faces inflation shock twice as large as U.S., TS Lombard's Perkins says
data.description:
Europe is facing an inflation shock about double that of the U.S. amid the deepening war in Iran, TS Lombard economist Dario Perkins said on Wednesday.

European natural gas prices surged more than 40% Tuesday following a 40% jump on Monday, as the production halt at the world's largest liquefied natural gas export facility in Qatar sparks fears over global supply and risks to energy security in Europe and Asia.

"Right now, Europe is looking at an inflation shock roughly double that of the US ~ 2%pts," Perkins wrote in a social media post.

Europe is more exposed than the U.S. as it imports most of its natural gas and relies heavily on global LNG markets, while the U.S. produces far more of its own energy.

Perkins told Seeking Alpha that "Europe's shock is much larger because nat gas prices are up much more in Europe." Meanwhile, "UK gas prices are up 90% but they are capped until July, luckily."

Meanwhile, Euro Area inflation rose 1.9% Y/Y in February, up from January’s 16-month low of 1.7% and above market expectations of 1.7%, according to a preliminary estimate released this week. Core inflation, which excludes energy, food, alcohol, and tobacco, rose to 2.4%, rebounding from January’s more than four-year low of 2.2%.

Natural gas ETFs: (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), and (FCG)

Oil ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), and (USL)

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC).

data.title: Ocugen outlines 2026 BLA submission and expects three pivotal filings within three years amid leadership expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Ocugen (OCGN) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Shankar Musunuri stated, "I'm pleased to share an update on what was a transformative year for Ocugen. Considerable development across all of our modified gene therapy programs, including licensing and financing agreements to strengthen our financial position and meaningful appointments to our leadership team made in 2025 a year of real momentum for Ocugen." He highlighted the completion of enrollment for the OCU400 Phase III liMeliGhT trial, noting it is the "only broad RP gene-agnostic trial and the largest known Phase III orphan gene therapy trial." Musunuri reported, "We are on track to begin the rolling BLA submission in the third quarter of 2026."
  • Musunuri announced the first regional licensing agreement for OCU400 in Korea, noting, "With upfront fees and near-term development milestone payments, along with royalties, this was a valuable collaboration for Ocugen and a critical step in the company's business development strategy."
  • On OCU410ST, Musunuri emphasized, "The Phase II/III GARDian3 pivotal confirmatory trial remains ahead of schedule. We anticipate top line Phase II/III data in the second quarter of 2027, followed by the BLA submission."
  • Musunuri described OCU410’s Phase II data as "compelling", with plans to initiate Phase III in 2026 following a full data set release.
  • New executive appointments were highlighted, including Abhi Gupta as EVP, Commercial and Business Development, Rita Johnson-Greene as CFO, and Paul Staid as EVP, Operations.
  • CFO Rita Johnson-Greene stated, "Research and development expenses for the 3 months ended December 31, 2025, were $10.7 million compared to $8.3 million for the 3 months ended December 31, 2024...Ocugen reported a $0.06 net loss per common share for the 3 months ended December 31, 2025, compared to a $0.05 net loss per common share for the 3 months ended December 31, 2024. Our current cash and cash equivalents extend our runway into the fourth quarter of 2026. This includes the recent raise of $22.5 million through an underwritten registered direct offering of common stock led by RTW Investments."

Outlook

  • The company reiterated the goal to file three BLAs in the next three years. Musunuri projected, "We are on track to begin the rolling BLA submission in the third quarter of 2026," with OCU400 commercialization anticipated in 2027.
  • For OCU410ST, top line Phase II/III data is anticipated in the second quarter of 2027, with BLA submission to follow. OCU410’s Phase III trial is expected to initiate in 2026, and full Phase II data for OCU410 will be reported this month. The company also expects interim pivotal data for OCU410ST in the third quarter and to complete enrollment for OCU410ST in early 2026.

Financial Results

  • Research and development expenses for Q4 2025 were $10.7 million, and general and administrative expenses were $6.1 million. The net loss per common share was $0.06 for the quarter.
  • For the full year 2025, research and development expenses totaled $39.8 million, and general and administrative expenses were $27.6 million, with a net loss per share of $0.23.
  • Cash and cash equivalents provide a runway into Q4 of 2026, with potential extension to Q2 2027 if warrants are exercised.

Q&A

  • Michael Okunewitch, Maxim Group: Asked about confidence in data turnaround for the liMeliGhT study and modifications to the primary endpoint. Huma Qamar, Chief Medical Officer, responded, "We are very confident that we will be able to hit our time line," and explained the sensitivity and specificity of the Luminance Dependent Navigation Assessment as a validated and FDA-aligned endpoint.
  • Okunewitch asked about competition in Stargardt disease. Musunuri responded, "We're not worried about other therapies if they come to the market first...we believe we are going to set the standard of care for Stargardt patients globally."
  • Boris Peaker, Titan Partners: Queried about FDA feedback on CMC filing and the ellipsoid zone as an endpoint. Musunuri noted, "Typically, the CMC will be -- also we are planning to file this year...If you hit the primary endpoint, you'll get the approval. If you hit the secondary, yes, you can include it in the product insert and the label."
  • Swayampakula Ramakanth, H.C. Wainwright: Asked about dose selection and eligibility criteria for OCU410 Phase III. Musunuri advised waiting for the complete data set to inform decisions.
  • Elemer Piros, Lucid Capital: Inquired about clinically meaningful improvement thresholds. Qamar stated, "Technically, one lux level and more because it's a validated protocol LDNA...the clinically meaningfulness is greater than or equal to 1 lux level."
  • Steven Klupt, Chardan: Asked about responder characteristics in OCU410. Qamar explained, "There was not really any other unique criteria that we would say at this point till we get our final clinical study report."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was neutral to slightly positive, focusing on trial design, regulatory alignment, and competitive positioning. Questions were probing, with an emphasis on timelines, endpoints, and differentiation.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone, frequently using language such as "we are very confident" and "compelling," and emphasizing alignment with regulatory authorities. There was no indication of defensive or evasive responses in the prepared remarks or Q&A.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remained steady, with continued focus on clinical and regulatory progress, but increased emphasis on commercialization and leadership expansion.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management guidance shifted from a focus on enrollment and interim data in Q3 to a concrete timeline for BLA submissions and commercial launch in Q4.
  • Strategic focus expanded to include both clinical milestones and operational readiness, with new executive appointments.
  • Analysts in both quarters concentrated on trial design and regulatory processes but were more focused on commercialization and competitive positioning in the current call.
  • Financially, net loss per share and expenses increased slightly quarter-over-quarter, with a recent capital raise bolstering the cash runway.
  • Management’s confidence in timelines and regulatory alignment was consistent, but more detail was provided on commercialization plans and partnership strategy in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged the inherent risks in regulatory timelines, noting that statements are "subject to risks, uncertainties and assumptions that may cause actual outcomes to differ materially."
  • Q&A highlighted possible competition in Stargardt disease, trial endpoint choices, and the need for ongoing FDA alignment as potential areas of risk.
  • The cash runway is dependent on warrant exercises for extension beyond Q4 2026.

Final Takeaway

Ocugen’s Q4 2025 call underscored a pivotal year for the company, marked by clinical milestone progress, a strengthened leadership team, and readiness for late-stage regulatory activities. With three BLA filings targeted over the next three years and a clear path to commercialization for OCU400 in 2027, management emphasized confidence in clinical trial endpoints, regulatory engagement, and financial positioning to support its transition into a commercial-stage biotechnology business.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Sphere representatives are in early talks about a Nashville venue - Axios
data.description:
Sphere Entertainment Co. (SPHR) has held talks with local businesspeople in Nashville about building a smaller version of the company's entertainment venue in Music City, according to Axios.

The company has already announced plans for a second Sphere in Prince George's County, Maryland, that would have a smaller capacity than the Las Vegas site of just 6,000 concertgoers. In broad terms, Sphere Entertainment executive chairman and CEO James Dolan has reiterated the company has a goal to expand Sphere's global footprint.

Nashville solidified its reputation as a music capital with the launch of the Grand Ole Opry in 1925, which established it as the epicenter of country music. By the 1950s and 60s, the city developed the polished “Nashville Sound” and became a major recording hub with the rise of Music Row. Beyond country, the city has a deep history in R&B and is considered a diverse music hub, with live music defining its culture.

Shares of Sphere Entertainment Co. (SPHR) were up 3.1% in Wednesday afternoon trading to $114.90 vs. the 52-week range of $23.89 to $120.27. Short interest on SPHR stands at 26.1% of the total float as some traders eye the valuation metrics after a strong six-month rally.

data.title: Wix outlines mid-teens revenue growth for 2026 as AI strategy accelerates, Base44 surpasses $100M ARR
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Wix.com Ltd. (WIX) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Avishai Abrahami, CEO, stated that "2026 is shaping up to be a defining year and the start of a new chapter shape, not just by the continued shift towards AI, but by AI beginning to prove its real-world value and utility more broadly." He emphasized that the acquisition of Base44 has expanded Wix's total addressable market significantly, with users now able to build tailored software applications and smart mobile apps. The CEO highlighted the launch of Wix Harmony, an AI-driven website creation platform, noting early performance is "particularly across conversion and monetization metrics."
  • Nir Zohar, President and COO, reported "new cohort bookings maintained double-digit growth in the fourth quarter," and Base44's user base is scaling rapidly, with "the number of new users joining Base44 is nearly 2/3 of the number of new users joining Wix." He announced the company plans to complete the majority of its $2 billion repurchase program in 2026 and referenced a $250 million equity investment from Durable Capital Partners as "a powerful endorsement of our long-term vision."
  • Lior Shemesh, CFO, said, "Total bookings in Q4 was $535 million, up 15% year-over-year, while total revenue was $524 million, up 14% year-over-year." He added, "Base44 finished the year with approximately $59 million of ARR above our expectations at the time of acquisition," and noted Base44 reached approximately $100 million in ARR. The CFO explained, "We exited the year with free cash flow of $156 million in Q4 or 30% of revenue."

Outlook

  • Lior Shemesh stated, "For the full year 2026, we expect bookings and revenue for the consolidated business to grow at mid-teens percentage year-over-year." He added, "For the first quarter of 2026, we expect revenue for the consolidated business to grow at a mid-teens percentage on a year-over-year basis."
  • The CFO also shared, "For the full year 2026, we expect free cash flow margin in the low to mid-20% range, assuming current capital structure and excluding acquisition expenses." The company indicated this guidance range is wider than usual due to the "dynamic hyper-growth trajectory of Base44."

Financial Results

  • Total bookings in Q4 were $535 million, up 15% year-over-year, and total revenue was $524 million, up 14% year-over-year, according to CFO Shemesh.
  • Base44 finished the year with approximately $59 million of ARR and recently reached approximately $100 million in ARR.
  • GPV for the quarter was $3.7 billion, up 11% year-over-year, and transaction revenue grew 18% in Q4.
  • Partners revenue was $203 million in Q4, growing 21% year-over-year.
  • Non-GAAP gross margin for Q4 was 68%, and non-GAAP operating income was 15% of revenue.
  • Free cash flow in Q4 was $156 million, or 30% of revenue, and for the full year, $605 million, or 30% of revenue.

Q&A

  • Bradley Erickson, RBC Capital Markets: Asked about key contributors to organic growth in the bookings guide and the mix between payment volumes, subscriber shift, or pricing. CFO Shemesh responded, "We don't assume any increase in pricing or change in pricing... the entire guidance that we provided for 2026 actually reflects the growth that we see within the Wix core business and Base44."
  • Josh Beck, Raymond James: Questioned the upmarket trend for Base44 and conversion rates among free users. President Zohar stated, "Harmony is a product we built for the self-creators... For Base44... the range of opportunities in terms of the kind of usage is still extremely wide."
  • Hoi-Fung Wong, Oppenheimer: Inquired about what accelerated Base44's growth and the bookings mix for 2026. CFO Shemesh replied, "We do see an acceleration and in a way, also better than what we expected... A big part of Base44 growth is actually based on the synergy with Wix."
  • Deepak Mathivanan, Cantor Fitzgerald: Asked about Harmony's initial user profile and the OpenAI partnership. CEO Abrahami explained, "We're pretty much seeing everybody using Harmony that was using Wix before... As for OpenAI, it's in intelligence in Wix talking to the intelligence in OpenAI... this creates tremendous opportunities for the future."
  • Trevor Young, Barclays: Questioned premium sub declines and growth composition. President Zohar explained the strategic shift: "We prefer to go after higher-value subscriptions... you see the lower net subs, but you're seeing the higher value that comes into the cohorts over time."
  • Mark Zgutowicz, Benchmark: Inquired about Harmony's cohort KPIs and LLM partnership economics. CFO Shemesh said, "We see a better conversion, faster monetization and also higher ARPU" for Harmony cohorts.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was generally inquisitive, focusing on the durability of Base44’s growth, cohort dynamics, and the sustainability of margin trends. There was pressing on user segmentation, monetization, and risks of margin pressure.
  • Management expressed confidence during prepared remarks and Q&A, using phrases such as "we believe that our current stock performance greatly undervalues these opportunities" (President Zohar) and "we are confident in our strategy, our ability to execute and the opportunity in front of us" (CEO Abrahami). Tone was slightly more cautious when discussing cost and margin variability related to Base44’s rapid growth.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone appeared more assertive and optimistic, particularly regarding the company’s AI-driven strategy and Base44’s momentum. Analysts remained consistently probing on sustainability and margins.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to a "broader range of potential outcomes" for 2026, reflecting the unpredictable trajectory of Base44.
  • Strategic focus advanced from anticipation of new AI products to the launch and early international rollout of Wix Harmony and accelerated Base44 integration.
  • The scale and ambition of the stock repurchase program increased, with intent to complete the majority of the $2 billion program in 2026, compared to a more incremental approach previously.
  • Key metrics indicated accelerating revenue, bookings, and ARR at Base44, with explicit reference to $100 million in ARR, a notable jump from prior quarter’s figures.
  • Management confidence in AI product adoption and synergy between Base44 and core Wix business increased, while analysts maintained focus on margin headwinds and cohort quality.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited "continued GPV headwinds as SMBs on the platform saw macro pressure" and seasonally softer GPV than anticipated.
  • Elevated AI compute costs and marketing investments for Base44 are causing "front-loaded" cost pressure, which management expects to moderate as the product matures and conversions improve.
  • Currency headwinds from the weakening U.S. dollar against the Israeli shekel were flagged as a material impact on payroll expenses.
  • The rapid growth of Base44 introduces variability in free cash flow margin, as acknowledged in the wider-than-typical guidance range for 2026.

Final Takeaway

Wix’s management sees 2026 as a pivotal year of accelerated growth, driven by the successful rollout of Wix Harmony and the rapid scaling of Base44, which has surpassed $100 million in ARR. The company anticipates mid-teens percentage growth in bookings and revenue, supported by strong new cohort momentum and deepening AI integration. Leadership remains confident in its ability to capture the expanding market opportunity, while acknowledging near-term risks around margin variability and macro headwinds, and is committed to completing the majority of its $2 billion share repurchase program within the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Anthropic's investors attempt to soothe situation with Pentagon: report
data.description:
Several major investors in Anthropic (ANTHRO) are working to resolve the artificial intelligence startup's recent fallout with the Pentagon by appealing to company executives and through contacts in the White House, according to Reuters.

Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has discussed the matter with Amazon (AMZN) CEO Andy Jassy, one of the startup's biggest backers, the report said. Anthropic executives have been contacted by venture capital firms Lightspeed and Iconiq as well. What's more, some investors have reached out to contacts in the Trump Administration in an attempt to cool the fires.

The investors are concerned that the labeling of Anthropic as a "supply-chain risk" could affect the company's potential revenue well beyond federal government contracts.

U.S. President Donald Trump and U.S. Secretary of War Pete Hegseth have called for all government agencies to phase out the use of Anthropic within the next six months. They have also instructed all companies that contract with the military to cease any commercial interactions with Anthropic.

The situation has placed Amodei in a precarious predicament. On the one hand, some investors are proud Amodei took a stand against the Pentagon and refused to let its AI models run autonomous weapons systems or engage in widespread domestic surveillance, the report said. However, others worry the "supply-chain risk" label could severely impact revenue potential. 

Anthropic's rival OpenAI (OPENAI) was swift to swoop in and sign an agreement with the Pentagon to deploy its models within a classified government network after the fallout.

Still, Anthropic remains popular with users. Claude climbed to the top spot in Apple's (AAPL) ranking of free apps over the weekend, even as the company faces the ire of the Trump administration over its safety terms.

Anthropic has also argued that Hegseth does not have the statutory authority to restrict anyone who does business with the military from doing business with Anthropic.

data.title: OpenAI CEO says company can't tell Pentagon how to use its AI tech
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) CEO Sam Altman told employees late Tuesday that the company can't tell the Pentagon how to use its AI technology.

“So maybe you think the Iran strike was good and the Venezuela invasion was bad,” Altman told OpenAI employees during a company meeting, according to CNBC. “You don’t get to weigh in on that.”

Altman added that operational decisions about how the military uses OpenAI's technology will ultimately be made by War Secretary Pete Hegseth. OpenAI, best known for its ChatGPT AI models, announced an agreement with the Pentagon last week that allows for its AI tech to be deployed across classified systems.

The CEO also noted that xAI (X.AI), which is run by Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk, recently signed a deal that allows the Pentagon to use its Grok AI technology in classified systems.

“I believe we will hopefully have the best models that will encourage the government to be willing to work with us, even if our safety stack annoys them,” Altman said at the employee meeting. “But there will be at least one other actor, which I assume will be xAI, which effectively will say, ‘We’ll do whatever you want.’”

Altman's remarks come in the wake of rival Anthropic (ANTHRO) refusing to allow the Pentagon to access its Claude AI technology without assurances that it wouldn't be used for mass domestic surveillance or fully autonomous weapons. The Trump administration responded by “blacklisting” Anthropic, barring government agencies from using its technology and government contractors from doing business with the company. 

Anthropic is backed by Amazon (AMZN) and Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL). Alphabet's Google is reportedly in negotiations with the Pentagon over the use of its Gemini AI models. OpenAI, which was co-founded with Musk, is backed by Microsoft (MSFT). 

Musk's xAI (X.AI), which also owns X, recently merged with his SpaceX (SPACE) transportation company, which also owns Starlink (STRLK). XAI reportedly plans to go public later this year.

data.title: Uber extends rally for a seventh straight session
data.description:
Shares of Uber Technologies (UBER) rose 0.58% to $76.80 in the afternoon trade on Wednesday, adding to a six-session winning streak.

The stock has gained around 7% between February 24 and March 3 compared to a 1.07% fall of the S&P 500 during the same period.

The move comes amid recent developments, including coverage of urban policy shifts after a judge ruled the Trump administration’s move to end New York City’s congestion toll was unlawful, continued attention on autonomous driving after Wayve raised $1.2B from backers including Microsoft, Nvidia, and Uber, and updates on Uber’s push into aerial mobility alongside Joby. The company also announced plans to acquire parking app SpotHero, adding to strategic expansion efforts during the period.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, UBER is rated Hold, with a score of 3.12 out of 5, receiving an A+ for profitability, but offset by a D- in terms of momentum.

A Seeking Alpha analysis said Uber has become a volatile “battleground” stock as investors debate whether autonomous vehicles represent a threat or tailwind, adding that “the unit economics of AV technology could offer a tailwind for Uber Technologies.”

The analyst noted that “70% of contra-revenue… expenses are driver-related,” arguing that eliminating human-driver costs could lift profitability, and pointed out that Uber’s “200 million+ user network” gives it leverage with AV partners. The note also highlighted diversification, stating that “approximately 50% of total revenue is generated internationally” and “approximately 50% of total gross bookings is generated through the delivery segment.”

On Wall Street, 47 of 56 analysts rate the stock Buy or higher, eight recommend Hold, and one recommends a Strong Sell.

Shares have gained around 1.85% in the past one month, and is down approximately 7.15% year-to-date.

data.title: Netflix shares uptick for seven consecutive sessions; add over 25% in last six sessions
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) shares clocked seven straight sessions of gains, as the stock rose 1.2% at $98.92 on Wednesday.

The movies and entertainment giant gained 25.2% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has added over 5% in the year.

NFLX is up 19% over the past one month.

Netflix abruptly withdrew from its bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) in late February, many in Hollywood were caught off guard. 

Most recently, Netflix announced that it is expanding its Ads Suite to give brands more options to buy and measure ads, with a focus on better targeting, frequency management, and scaled reach on its ad-supported plan. 

"Management’s disciplined approach avoids integration risk, preserves balance sheet strength, and positions Netflix for gradual, compelling standalone improvements," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, NFLX has a Buy rating with a score of 3.1 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, while it received D- in valuation.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 36 analysts gave NFLX a Buy and above rating. 11 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while one recommended Sell.

 
 

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.
